title: Evaluating Cross-Cutting Prevention Themes for Use in Military Settings
authors_full: Rachel Larson, PhD; Amanda Straus, DBH; Alondra Cruz, MA; Scott Kahldon, MBA; Kayla Stevenson; & Jocelyn Magana

summary.hook: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness.
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness. The team used a three-phase approach (Discovery → Implementation → Evaluation) that included interviews, coding themes, and creating site-specific implementation plans and prevention activities.

audience_versions.clinician: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness. The team conducted 100 interviews with members of the military sites to inform needs/resources and tailored project development. For clinicians, the main value is whether the findings could inform care decisions, screening, workflow, or patient communication.
audience_versions.community: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness. “Continuing prevention efforts is essential as military readiness rates decline nationwide.” For community partners, the key question is who benefits, who might be left out, and how the work could translate into real-world impact.
audience_versions.leader: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness. “Continuing prevention efforts is essential as military readiness rates decline nationwide.” For leaders, the main lens is whether this work is ready for scale, support, implementation, or further investment within digital health & research technology.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster describes a collaboration with four military sites to assess and strengthen prevention strategies intended to improve military readiness.
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Mixed methods / implementation-oriented evaluation (interviews \+ thematic coding \+ site-specific prevention activity development; evaluation ongoing). Four military sites; 100...
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: The team conducted 100 interviews with members of the military sites to inform needs/resources and tailored project development.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: “Continuing prevention efforts is essential as military readiness rates decline nationwide.”
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: In Figure 1, what specific deliverables were produced during Discovery besides interviews (e.g., site implementation plans—what format)?

takeaways: The team conducted 100 interviews with members of the military sites to inform needs/resources and tailored project development.
Interview coding results show theme counts: Universal = 149, Selective = 83, Indicated = 71 (bar chart).
“Continuing prevention efforts is essential as military readiness rates decline nationwide.”
